title: Quantum Advantage Tracker: the race to advantage | IBM Quantum Computing Blog
why:
- The open-source Quantum Advantage Tracker allows researchers to compare quantum and classical computing methods in real time, supporting faster, collaborative progress toward verifying quantum advantage.
- Over 30 submissions from leading institutions have been made to the Tracker, including experiments run on IBM and Quantinuum hardware, highlighting broad community engagement.
- Continuous, rapid-fire exchanges on the Tracker enable updates and refinements to experiments much faster than traditional scientific publishing cycles.
- Recent experiments with "peaked circuits" demonstrated scenarios where quantum hardware drastically outperformed classical methods, but subsequent classical algorithm improvements quickly closed the gap, emphasizing the need for ongoing, open benchmarking.
- The Tracker is fostering a collaborative environment where quantum advantage is pursued through transparent, rigorous validation and iterative challenge between quantum and classical approaches.
